headlines.text: Iran Re-Closes Strait of Hormuz Hours After Reopening It, Accusing US of 'Piracy'
Trump Says US Naval Blockade Will 'Remain in Full Force' Until Iran Deal Is Done
S&P 500 Closes at Record 7,126 as Markets Rally on Iran Peace Hopes
Democrat Analilia Mejia Wins NJ-11 Special Election, Narrows GOP House Majority to 218-214
Madonna Surprises Crowd at Coachella Weekend 2, Joins Sabrina Carpenter Onstage
Senate Passes Short-Term Surveillance Program Extension Through April 30
Gas Prices Hit $4.08 National Average, Up Nearly 30% from a Year Ago
Trump Warns Iran: 'We'll Have to Start Dropping Bombs Again' If No Deal Reached
Pope Leo XIV Pushes Back on Trump Criticism, Calls for Peace in Iran War
Netflix Co-Founder Reed Hastings to Leave Board in June as Earnings Disappoint

breaking.text: BREAKING: Iran's IRGC re-closes Strait of Hormuz, says it has returned to 'previous state' under strict military control after US refuses to lift port blockade

stories.hed: Iran Opens Strait, Iran Closes Strait, Trump Threatens Bombs — All Before Lunch
stories.dek: In a dizzying 24-hour whiplash, Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz on Friday, causing oil prices to crater 9% and Wall Street to throw a party. Then on Saturday morning, the IRGC slammed it shut again, accusing the US of 'piracy' for maintaining its naval blockade of Iranian ports. Trump responded by saying the blockade stays until the deal is '100% done' — and that bombs are back on the menu if talks fail.

stories.hed: Pope Leo XIV and Trump Are in an Actual Feud Over the Iran War
stories.dek: Pope Leo XIV, on his Africa tour, called out a 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the US-Israel war in Iran. Trump fired back on social media accusing Leo of being 'weak on crime and foreign policy.' The Vatican responded by noting its peace appeals are rooted in the Gospel — which, one presumes, Trump has not read lately.

stories.hed: Progressive Analilia Mejia Wins NJ-11, Squeezes GOP House Majority to a Nail-Biting 218-214
stories.dek: Democrat and former Bernie Sanders campaign staffer Analilia Mejia crushed Republican Joe Hathaway in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election Thursday, further narrowing Republicans' already paper-thin House majority. Democrats have now overperformed in multiple special elections since Trump returned to office, which is making the November midterms look very interesting indeed.

stories.hed: Congress Punts on Spy Program with Last-Minute Extension Through April 30
stories.dek: After failing to pass either a 5-year renewal or Trump's preferred 18-month version, the Senate passed a short-term fix extending the warrantless overseas surveillance program by just 13 days. Congress has essentially scheduled a second cliffhanger for itself in two weeks, which is on brand.

stories.hed: Wall Street Hit an All-Time Record This Week Entirely on Vibes and Presidential Optimism
stories.dek: The S&P 500 closed Friday at a record 7,126 despite the Strait of Hormuz being, at best, open in spirit. Traders essentially priced in a peace deal that does not yet exist, based on Trump's assurances — which is either genius forward-looking analysis or the most expensive act of collective credulity in market history. Oil then proceeded to get re-closed overnight, so.

markets.context: Markets surged Friday on Iran peace optimism and Hormuz reopening news, but oil cratered 9% — then Iran re-closed the strait Saturday morning, which the market has not fully digested yet.

gas.note: Paying $4 a gallon to drive to a job that has not given you a raise since 2022 is called the American experience.

popCulture.hed: Madonna Crashes Sabrina Carpenter's Coachella Set and the Internet Has Not Recovered
popCulture.dek: Madonna rose from beneath the Coachella stage during Sabrina Carpenter's Weekend 2 headlining set, launching into 'Vogue' and 'Like a Prayer' in what is already being called the surprise of the festival season. The Material Girl appearing under the stage while Gen Z screamed is either a metaphor for something or just extremely good television.

genZ.term: Rick and Morty Dance / I Was Looking For a Job
genZ.definition: An AI-generated cartoon dance battle video originally posted by TikToker @reqdeca went viral in early April 2026 after @diarymarthy_ set it to a Yeat-style remix of The Smiths' 'Heaven Knows I'm Miserable Now' — specifically the line 'I was looking for a job and then I found a job.' The format exploded into memes pairing the shuffling cartoon characters with brainrot captions about mundane suffering.
genZ.usage: Congress passing a 13-day surveillance extension instead of a real bill is giving very 'I was looking for a job and then I found a job... that expires April 30' energy.

tldr: Iran opened the Strait of Hormuz, Wall Street partied at record highs, then Iran closed it again — all within about 18 hours, which is a reasonable summary of 2026 so far. Meanwhile, Democrats won another special election, Madonna showed up at Coachella, and your gas is still $4 a gallon.
